draft.title: ai workspace
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
ai workspace

Building your first draft…

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer quantified metrics and KPIs over narrative summaries
facade.label: Dashboard with numbers
facade.acceptImplies: Build prominent metric displays, gauges, counters—prioritize scannable numbers
facade.rejectImplies: Lean toward narrative insight cards, trend stories, explanatory text over raw figures

evidence.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer quantified metrics and KPIs over narrative summaries
evidence.implication: Build prominent metric displays, gauges, counters—prioritize scannable numbers

facade.hypothesis: An ai workspace benefits from low-light color schemes with high contrast and uncluttered layouts to reduce cognitive load during extended focus work
facade.axisTargeted: Light vs. Dark + Complexity vs. Simplicity
facade.label: Dark, minimal interface
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers calm, distraction-free environments with reduced visual noise and dark mode aesthetics
facade.rejectImplies: User may favor brighter, more colorful, or content-rich interfaces with higher visual information density

facade.hypothesis: Users building AI workspaces prefer soft, ambient light elements over harsh fluorescent aesthetics
facade.label: Warm accent lighting
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should use warm color temperatures, gentle glows, and soft shadows to create psychological comfort alongside computational intensity
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace prioritizes clarity and efficiency over atmospheric warmth; cool, bright, clinical lighting is acceptable or even preferred

facade.hypothesis: An AI workspace that speaks in a relaxed, conversational voice will feel more approachable and less intimidating than formal corporate language
facade.label: Casual chat tone
facade.acceptImplies: Users want their AI tools to feel like helpful colleagues or collaborators, not distant enterprise software. Embrace conversational phrasing, warmth, and personality in copy and interactions.
facade.rejectImplies: Users expect professional polish and authority in an AI workspace. Casual language might undermine credibility. Lean into clarity, precision, and formal tone instead.

facade.hypothesis: Users prefer fluid, eased motion over instant state changes in workspace interactions
facade.label: Smooth transitions
facade.acceptImplies: The workspace should prioritize animated transitions, ease curves, and graceful movement between states (panels opening, focus shifts, element reveals)
facade.rejectImplies: The workspace should favor instant, zero-latency interactions with minimal or no motion—snappy click-response over fluid choreography

facade.hypothesis: AI workspaces benefit from a persistent left navigation paired with modular card-based content areas rather than tabbed or list-based layouts
facade.label: Sidebar + cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers spatial organization with dedicated nav zones and independent, stackable content blocks — likely values quick context-switching and visual hierarchy
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer tab-based organization, full-width flows, or list views — suggesting they want linear task sequences or maximal screen real estate per task

axes.label: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers minimal, clean interfaces vs. rich visual detail and color
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants tools always visible vs. hidden/revealed on demand
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, conversational guidance vs. neutral, direct instruction
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user seeks calm, distraction-free focus vs. energetic, stimulating environment
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers flat, simple information hierarchy vs. layered, detailed organization
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants instant feedback and snappy responses vs. smooth, considered transitions
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: AI workspace users prefer chronological narrative flows and progressive disclosure over static dashboard snapshots
facade.axisTargeted: Information architecture — snapshot dashboards vs. temporal sequences
facade.label: Scrolling timeline
facade.acceptImplies: Design around time-based feeds, activity streams, and progressive reveals. Prioritize 'what happened when' over 'what is the current state'
facade.rejectImplies: Reinforces preference for static, all-at-once overview. Double down on simultaneous metric visibility and spatial layouts over temporal ordering

draft.title: AI Workspace Hub
draft.summary: A focused workspace interface for managing AI tasks, projects, and collaborative workflows. Clean, minimal design with warm neutrals and intuitive navigation.
